rationale: This reaction represents the classic Pomeranz-Fritsch synthesis of the parent isoquinoline from benzaldehyde and aminoacetaldehyde diethyl acetal (represented here as 2-amino-1,1-diethoxyethane, NC(CO)CO). The reaction proceeds via formation of a Schiff base (or benzalaminoacetal), followed by acid-catalyzed cyclization onto the aromatic ring and subsequent elimination of ethanol to form the isoquinoline core. This example uses the simplest possible aromatic aldehyde (benzaldehyde) and aminoacetal, making it a foundational and canonical representation of the transformation.
collision_avoidance_note: This example uses simple, unsubstituted benzaldehyde and an aminoacetal to form the parent isoquinoline. This specific transformation is highly characteristic of the Pomeranz-Fritsch reaction. It avoids complex polycyclic systems, heteroaromatic aldehydes, or additional functional groups that could overlap with other named reactions involving aromatic aldehydes (e.g., Bischler-Napieralski, Pictet-Spengler, which require activated rings and different amine partners) or general condensation reactions.
